feedback.text: I truly believe that, in this digital age, our right to ownership is danger as things are right now. With companies all over shutting down access to things people have purchased. This goes for cars, washing machines, printers, etc. Where the company selling it has it connected to the internet, and are able to remotely kill the item whenever they feel like it. On top of many companies constantly changing the terms of the sale with their terms of service. This is highly anti consumer. One area that has a huge problem when it comes to ownership, is video games. A market where most sales are done digitally, but where most companies insist on the idea of being able to delete access to said games at their leisure. It is not only frustrating, but also like them stealing your game from you after a legal purchase of the game.
